---
title: "Tests for variable importance estimation"
author: "Stacy Cyrille"
date: last-modified
format: 
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  # html:
  #   keep-md: false
  #   toc: true
  #   toc-location: left
  #   number-sections: true
  #   embed-resources: true
execute: 
  # echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
fig-width: 8
fig-align: "center"
fig-cap-location: top
html-math-method: mathml
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(MASS)
library(knitr)
library(survival)
library(timereg)
library(survML)
library(survivalSL)
library(SuperLearner)
library(randomForestSRC)
library(survex)

source(here::here("R", "utils.R"))
source(here::here("R", "generate_data.R"))
source(here::here("R", "pred_generators.R"))

# Set seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(2026)
# n <- 200000
n <- 500
V <- 5
sample_split <- TRUE
vims <- c("AUC","brier")
R <- 10
# R <- 1000
```

# Scenario 1: Additive hazards 

## Objectives

This scenario: 

* evaluates the overall performance of the VIM procedure under additive hazards: bias, confidence interval coverage, confidence interval width, rejection probability 
* assesses the robustness of the VIM procedure to nuisance-model choice required for VIM computation: Aalen model (`timereg::aalen`), survival Super Learner  (`survivalSL::survivalSL`), global survival stacking (`survML::stackG`)

## Data generation 

Four binary covariates were generated *independently* with prevalences spanning 
rare to frequent alterations, $X_j \sim Bernoulli(0.4)$. 

Events times were simulated under the following the additive hazards 
model: 

$$\lambda(t|x)=\lambda_0(t)+\alpha_{1}(t)X_{1}+\alpha_{2}(t)X_{2}+\alpha_{3}(t)X_{3}+\alpha_{4}(t)X_{4}$$

with $\lambda_0(t)=0.05$ (constant) and $\alpha_j(t)=(0.1, 0.05, 0.005, 0)$. 

Independent censoring times were generated as $C \sim Uniform(0, c_{max})$. The
parameter $c_{max}$ was calibrated using a large Monte Carlo sample from the 
data-generating mechanism to achieve an expected censoring proportion of 
approximately 20%, and the calibrated value was then fixed across all 
simulation replicates. 

```{r}
p <- 4
sigma <- diag(x = 1, nrow = p, ncol = p)
prev <- rep(0.4, p)
alpha <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0.005, 0)
lambda0 <- 0.05
target_cens <- 0.2
c_max <- calibrate_c_max_aalen(lambda0     = lambda0,
                               alpha       = alpha,
                               prev        = prev, 
                               sigma       = sigma,
                               target_cens = target_cens,
                               seed        = 2026)
c_max
data_scen1 <- simulate_aalen_data(n       = n , 
                                  prev    = prev, 
                                  sigma   = sigma, 
                                  lambda0 = lambda0, 
                                  alpha   = alpha, 
                                  c_max   = c_max) 
head(data_scen1)

# Check prevalences
round(colMeans(data_scen1 %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("X"))), 3)

# Check censoring rate 
mean(1 - data_scen1$event)

aalen_fit <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+X4, data = data_scen1)
summary(aalen_fit)
```

**Kaplan-Meier**

```{r }
list_form <- c("1",paste0("X",1:p))%>%
  purrr::map(~as.formula(paste("Surv(time,event)~", .x)))
plot_surv(formula = list_form, 
          data = data_scen1, 
          legend.title = c("", paste0("X",1:p)))
```

**Aalen model**

```{r}
# Fit the model
aalen_fit <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+X4, data = data_scen1)
summary(aalen_fit)
```

***Test for non-significant effects***

The model estimates the cumulative effects

$$
A_j(t)=\int_{0}^{t}\alpha_j(u)du.
$$

The reported *Supremum test* evaluates $H_0: A_j(t)=0$, meaning the covariate has no effect on the hazard over time. 

Interpretation of the results 

* Intercept: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[1], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Baseline hazard is clearly non zero
* $X_1$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[2], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Strong evidence that $X_1$ affects the hazard 
* $X_2$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[3], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Strong evidence that $X_2$ affects the hazard 
* $X_3$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[4], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ No evidence that $X_3$ affects the hazard 
* $X_4$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[5], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ No evidence that $X_4$ affects the hazard 

***Test for time invariant effects***

Interpretation of the results of the Kolmogorov–Smirnov test

Small p-values $rigtharrow$ evidence of time-varying effects. 

* Intercept: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[1], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Baseline hazard clearly varies over time
* $X_1$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[2], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Strong evidence that effect of $X_1$ changes over time
* $X_2$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[3], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Strong evidence that effect of $X_2$ changes over time
* $X_3$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[4], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ No strong evidence against constant effect
* $X_4$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[5], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ No evidence of time variation

```{r }
# Plot the non-parametric cumulative regression coefficients with 95% CI
plot(aalen_fit, ylim = c(-0.5,4))
```

---

We simulated $R=1000$ datasets. 

```{r}
test_aalen <- replicate(R, {
  # Generate data 
  data <- simulate_aalen_data(n, prev, sigma, lambda0, alpha, c_max)
  # Estimate prevalences 
  prev <- colMeans(data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("X")))%>%
    stats::setNames(paste0("prev_", names(.)))
  # Censoring rate 
  cens_rate <- stats::setNames(mean(1 - data$event), "cens_rate")
  # Fit Aalen model
  fit_obj <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+X4, data)
  # Tests
  pval_supremum <- fit_obj[["pval.testBeq0"]] 
  names(pval_supremum) <- paste0("pval_supremum_", names(pval_supremum)) 
  pval_KS <- fit_obj[["pval.testBeqC"]] 
  names(pval_KS) <- paste0("pval_KS_", names(pval_KS)) 
  pval_CvM <- fit_obj[["pval.testBeqC.is"]] 
  names(pval_CvM) <- paste0("pval_CvM_", names(pval_CvM))

  c(prev, cens_rate, pval_supremum, pval_KS, pval_CvM)
  }, simplify = F)%>%
  bind_rows

data.frame(colMeans(test_aalen))%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column()%>%
  kable(col.names = c("Name","Value"))

test_aalen %>%
  dplyr::select(starts_with("pval"))%>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(.x<0.05, 1, 0)))%>%
  dplyr::summarise_all(mean)%>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything())%>%
  kable(col.names = c("p-value","Rejection probability"))
```

## Variable importance 

We compare the following approaches for estimating the nuisance functions 
required for VIM computation:

* Aalen additive hazards model (`timereg::aalen`)
* Super Learner for survival prediction (`survivalSL::survivalSL`)
* Global survival stacking (`survML::stackG`)

We define time horizons, maximum follow-up time and times at which to approximate integrals.

```{r}
time <- data_scen1$time
event <- data_scen1$event
X <- data_scen1 %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("X")) 
surv_aalen_fit <- survival::survfit(survival::Surv(time, event)~1)
landmark_times <- quantile(surv_aalen_fit, 
                           probs = c(#0.25, 
                                     0.75))$quantile
landmark_times
approx_times <- sort(unique(c(0, time[event == 1], landmark_times)))
approx_times <- approx_times[approx_times <= max(landmark_times)]
length(approx_times)
```

We generated cross-fitting and sample-splitting folds.

```{r}
folds <- survML:::generate_folds(n, V, sample_split)
cf_folds <- folds$cf_folds
table(cf_folds)
ss_folds <- folds$ss_folds
table(ss_folds)
```

### Global survival stacking 

```{r}
V0_stackG_preds_scen1 <- CV_generate_full_predictions_landmark(
  time = time,
  event = event,
  X = X,
  landmark_times = landmark_times,
  approx_times = approx_times,
  nuisance = "stackG",
  cf_folds = cf_folds)
# str(V0_stackG_preds_scen1)

CV_full_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen1$CV_full_preds
CV_full_preds_train <- V0_stackG_preds_scen1$CV_full_preds_train
CV_S_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen1$CV_S_preds
CV_G_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen1$CV_G_preds

indxs <- 1:ncol(X)
vim_stackG_scen1 <- purrr::map_dfr(indxs, function(indx_i) {
  
  char_indx <- as.character(indx_i)
  indx <- as.numeric(strsplit(char_indx, split = ",")[[1]])
  
  CV_reduced_preds <- CV_generate_reduced_predictions_landmark(
    time = time,
    event = event,
    X = X,
    landmark_times = landmark_times,
    cf_folds = cf_folds,
    indx = indx,
    full_preds_train = CV_full_preds_train
  )
  
  purrr::map_dfr(vims, function(vim) {
    
    output <- switch(
      vim,
      brier = survML::vim_brier(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = CV_full_preds,
        fs_hat = CV_reduced_preds,
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      ),
      AUC = survML::vim_AUC(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = purrr::map(CV_full_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        fs_hat = purrr::map(CV_reduced_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      )
    )
    
    output %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(
        vim = vim,
        indx = char_indx
      )
  })
})

kable(vim_stackG_scen1 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "AUC")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large AUC","small AUC"))

kable(vim_stackG_scen1 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "brier")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large Brier","small Brier"))
```

### Aalen model

```{r}
V0_aalen_preds_scen1 <- CV_generate_full_predictions_landmark(
  time = time,
  event = event,
  X = X,
  landmark_times = landmark_times,
  approx_times = approx_times,
  nuisance = "aalen",
  cf_folds = cf_folds)
# str(V0_aalen_preds_scen1)

CV_full_preds <- V0_aalen_preds_scen1$CV_full_preds
CV_full_preds_train <- V0_aalen_preds_scen1$CV_full_preds_train
CV_S_preds <- V0_aalen_preds_scen1$CV_S_preds
CV_G_preds <- V0_aalen_preds_scen1$CV_G_preds

indxs <- 1:ncol(X)
vim_aalen_scen1 <- purrr::map_dfr(indxs, function(indx_i) {
  
  char_indx <- as.character(indx_i)
  indx <- as.numeric(strsplit(char_indx, split = ",")[[1]])
  
  CV_reduced_preds <- CV_generate_reduced_predictions_landmark(
    time = time,
    event = event,
    X = X,
    landmark_times = landmark_times,
    cf_folds = cf_folds,
    indx = indx,
    full_preds_train = CV_full_preds_train
  )
  
  purrr::map_dfr(vims, function(vim) {
    
    output <- switch(
      vim,
      brier = survML::vim_brier(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = CV_full_preds,
        fs_hat = CV_reduced_preds,
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      ),
      AUC = survML::vim_AUC(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = purrr::map(CV_full_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        fs_hat = purrr::map(CV_reduced_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      )
    )
    
    output %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(
        vim = vim,
        indx = char_indx
      )
  })
})

kable(vim_aalen_scen1 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "AUC")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large AUC","small AUC"))

kable(vim_aalen_scen1 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "brier")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large Brier","small Brier"))
```

### Survival Super Learner 

```{r eval=FALSE}
V0_survivalSL_preds_scen1 <- CV_generate_full_predictions_landmark(
  time = time,
  event = event,
  X = X,
  landmark_times = landmark_times,
  approx_times = approx_times,
  nuisance = "survivalSL",
  cf_folds = cf_folds)
# str(V0_stackG_preds_scen1)

CV_full_preds <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen1$CV_full_preds
CV_full_preds_train <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen1$CV_full_preds_train
CV_S_preds <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen1$CV_S_preds
CV_G_preds <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen1$CV_G_preds

indxs <- 1:ncol(X)
vim_survivalSL_scen1 <- purrr::map_dfr(indxs, function(indx_i) {
  
  char_indx <- as.character(indx_i)
  indx <- as.numeric(strsplit(char_indx, split = ",")[[1]])
  
  CV_reduced_preds <- CV_generate_reduced_predictions_landmark(
    time = time,
    event = event,
    X = X,
    landmark_times = landmark_times,
    cf_folds = cf_folds,
    indx = indx,
    full_preds_train = CV_full_preds_train
  )
  
  purrr::map_dfr(vims, function(vim) {
    
    output <- switch(
      vim,
      brier = survML::vim_brier(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = CV_full_preds,
        fs_hat = CV_reduced_preds,
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      ),
      AUC = survML::vim_AUC(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = purrr::map(CV_full_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        fs_hat = purrr::map(CV_reduced_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      )
    )
    
    output %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(
        vim = vim,
        indx = char_indx
      )
  })
})

kable(vim_survivalSL_scen1 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "AUC")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large AUC","small AUC"))

kable(vim_survivalSL_scen1 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "brier")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large Brier","small Brier"))
```

# Scenario 2: Mixed additive and multiplicative hazards

## Objectives 

This scenario: 

* evaluates the overall performance of the VIM procedure under mixed additive and multiplicative hazards: bias, confidence interval coverage, confidence interval width, rejection probability 
* assesses the robustness of the VIM procedure to nuisance-model choice required for VIM computation: Cox-Aalen model (`timereg::cox.aalen`), survival Super Learner (`survivalSL::survivalSL`), global survival stacking  (`survML::stackG`)

## Data generation

The covariates are generated *independently* as follows:
  
* $X_{1}, X_{2}, X_{3} \sim \mathcal{B}(0.4)$
* $Z_{1}, Z_{2} \sim \mathcal{B}(0.4)$
* $Z_{3} \sim \mathcal{N}(0,1)$

To generate mixed additive and multiplicative effects, event times were 
simulated under a Cox-Aalen model with constant baseline hazard: 
$\lambda_0(t)=0.05$. Additive effects were assigned to $X_1$, $X_2$ and $X_3$ with 
coefficients $\alpha_{1}=0.1$, $alpha_{2}=0.05$ and $\alpha_{3}=0$, respectively. 
Multiplicative (proportional hazards) effects were assigned to 
$Z_1$, $Z_2$ and $Z_3$ with coefficients $\beta_{1}=1$, $beta_{2}=0.5$ and
$\beta_{3}=0$, corresponding to hazard ratios of approximately 2.72, 1.65 and 1. 

Independent censoring times were generated as $C \sim Uniform(0, c_{max})$. The
parameter $c_{max}$ was calibrated using a large Monte Carlo sample from the 
data-generating mechanism to achieve an expected censoring proportion of 
approximately 20%, and the calibrated value was then fixed across all 
simulation replicates. 

```{r}
prev_add <- c(0.4, 0.4, 0.4)
sigma_add <- diag(x = 1, nrow = 3, ncol = 3)
alpha <- c(0.1, 0.05, 0)
prev_mult <- c(0.4, 0.4)
sigma_mult_bin <- diag(x = 1, nrow = 2, ncol = 2)
mean_mult <- 0
sigma_mult_cont <- diag(x = 1, nrow = 1, ncol = 1)
beta <- c(1, 0.5, 0)
lambda0 <- 0.05
target_cens <- 0.05
c_max <- calibrate_c_max_cox_aalen(lambda0         = lambda0,
                                   alpha           = alpha,
                                   beta            = beta,
                                   prev_add        = prev_add,
                                   sigma_add       = sigma_add,
                                   prev_mult       = prev_mult,
                                   mean_mult       = mean_mult,
                                   sigma_mult_bin  = sigma_mult_bin,
                                   sigma_mult_cont = sigma_mult_cont,
                                   target_cens     = target_cens,
                                   seed            = 2026)
c_max
data_scen2 <- simulate_cox_aalen_data(
  n, lambda0, alpha, beta,
  prev_add, sigma_add, prev_mult, mean_mult,
  sigma_mult_bin, sigma_mult_cont, c_max
)
head(data_scen2)

# Check prevalences
colMeans(data_scen2 %>% dplyr::select(X1:X3,Z1:Z2))

# Check mean and sd 
round(mean(data_scen2$Z3), 3)
sd(data_scen2$Z3)

# Check censoring rate 
mean(1 - data_scen2$event)

cox_aalen_fit <- timereg::cox.aalen(
  Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+prop(Z1)+prop(Z2)+prop(Z3), 
  data = data_scen2, n.sim = 1000, basesim = 1
)
summary(cox_aalen_fit)
```
**Kaplan-Meier**

```{r }
list_form <- c("1",paste0("X",1:3), paste0("Z",1:2))%>%
  purrr::map(~as.formula(paste("Surv(time,event)~", .x)))
plot_surv(formula = list_form, 
          data = data_scen2, 
          legend.title = c("", paste0("X",1:3), paste0("Z",1:2)))
```

**Cox-Aalen model**

```{r}
# Fit Cox-Aalen model 
cox_aalen_fit <- timereg::cox.aalen(
  Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+prop(Z1)+prop(Z2)+prop(Z3), 
  data = data_scen2, n.sim = 1000, basesim = 1)
summary(cox_aalen_fit)
```

***Test for non-significant effects***

* Intercept: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[1], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Baseline hazard is clearly non zero
* $X_1$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[2], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Strong evidence that $X_1$ affects the hazard 
* $X_2$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.testBeq0[3], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ No evidence that $X_2$ affects the hazard 

***Test for time invariant effects***

* Intercept: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[1], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Baseline hazard clearly varies over time
* $X_1$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[2], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Strong evidence that $X_1$ affects the hazard 
* $X_2$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.testBeqC[3], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ No evidence of time variation

***Test of proportionality***

* $X_3$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.Prop[1], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Compatible with a proportional effect
* $X_4$: `r gtsummary::style_pvalue(cox_aalen_fit$pval.Prop[2], prepend_p = TRUE)` $\rightarrow$ Compatible with a proportional effect

```{r }
# Plot additive Aalen terms
plot(cox_aalen_fit, ylim = c(-0.5,5))

# Plot multiplicative terms 
plot(cox_aalen_fit,  score= TRUE) 
```

---

We simulated $R=1000$ datasets. 

```{r}
test_cox_aalen <- replicate(R, {
  # Generate data 
  data <- simulate_cox_aalen_data(n, lambda0, alpha, beta,
                                  prev_add, sigma_add, prev_mult, mean_mult,
                                  sigma_mult_bin, sigma_mult_cont, c_max)
  # Estimate prevalences 
  prev <- colMeans(data %>% dplyr::select(X1:X3))%>%
    stats::setNames(paste0("prev_", names(.)))
  # Mean and sd Z3
  mean_sd_Z3 <- stats::setNames(c(mean(data$Z3), sd(data$Z3)),
                                c("mean_Z3","sd_Z3"))
  # Censoring rate 
  cens_rate <- stats::setNames(mean(1 - data$event), "cens_rate")
  # Fit Cox-Aalen model
  fit_obj <- timereg::cox.aalen(
    Surv(time, event)~X1+X2+X3+prop(Z1)+prop(Z2)+prop(Z3),
    data = data, n.sim = 1000, basesim = 1
  )
  # Additive terms 
  pval_supremum <- fit_obj[["pval.testBeq0"]] 
  names(pval_supremum) <- paste0("pval_supremum_", names(pval_supremum)) 
  pval_KS <- fit_obj[["pval.testBeqC"]] 
  names(pval_KS) <- paste0("pval_KS_", names(pval_KS)) 
  # pval_CvM <- fit_obj[["pval.testBeqC.is"]] 
  # names(pval_CvM) <- paste0("pval_CvM", names(pval_CvM))
  # Multiplicative terms 
  coef <- coef(fit_obj)[,"Coef."]
  # Test for proportional 
  pval_prop <- stats::setNames(
    fit_obj$pval.Prop, 
    gsub("prop\\(|\\)","", paste0("pval_prop_", names(fit_obj$pval.Prop)))
    )
  
  c(prev, mean_sd_Z3, cens_rate, 
    pval_supremum, pval_KS, #pval_CvM, 
    coef, pval_prop)
  
  }, simplify = F)%>%
  bind_rows()

data.frame(round(colMeans(test_cox_aalen),3))%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column()%>%
  kable(col.names = c("Name","Value"))

test_cox_aalen %>%
  dplyr::select(starts_with("pval"))%>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(.x<0.05, 1, 0)))%>%
  dplyr::summarise_all(mean)%>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything())%>%
  mutate(value = round(value, 3))%>%
  kable(col.names = c("p-value","Rejection probability"))
```

## Variable importance

We compare the following approaches for estimating the nuisance functions 
required for VIM computation:

* Cox-Aalen model (`timereg::cox.aalen`)
* Survival Super Learner (`survivalSL::survivalSL`)
* Global survival stacking (`survML::stackG`)

We define time horizons, maximum follow-up time and times at which to approximate integrals.

```{r}
time <- data_scen2$time
event <- data_scen2$event
X <- data_scen2 %>% dplyr::select(starts_with(c("X","Z")))
surv_cox_aalen_fit <- survival::survfit(survival::Surv(time, event)~1)
landmark_times <- quantile(surv_cox_aalen_fit, 
                           probs = c(#0.25, 
                                     0.75))$quantile
approx_times <- sort(unique(c(0, time[event == 1], landmark_times)))
approx_times <- approx_times[approx_times <= max(landmark_times)]
length(approx_times)
```

We generated cross-fitting and sample-splitting folds.

```{r}
folds <- survML:::generate_folds(n, V, sample_split)
cf_folds <- folds$cf_folds
table(cf_folds)
ss_folds <- folds$ss_folds
table(ss_folds)
```

### Global survival stacking 

```{r}
V0_stackG_preds_scen2 <- CV_generate_full_predictions_landmark(
  time = time,
  event = event,
  X = X,
  landmark_times = landmark_times,
  approx_times = approx_times,
  nuisance = "stackG",
  cf_folds = cf_folds)
# str(V0_stackG_preds_scen2)

CV_full_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen2$CV_full_preds
CV_full_preds_train <- V0_stackG_preds_scen2$CV_full_preds_train
CV_S_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen2$CV_S_preds
CV_G_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen2$CV_G_preds

indxs <- 1:ncol(X)
vim_stackG_scen2 <- purrr::map_dfr(indxs, function(indx_i) {
  
  char_indx <- as.character(indx_i)
  indx <- as.numeric(strsplit(char_indx, split = ",")[[1]])
  
  CV_reduced_preds <- CV_generate_reduced_predictions_landmark(
    time = time,
    event = event,
    X = X,
    landmark_times = landmark_times,
    cf_folds = cf_folds,
    indx = indx,
    full_preds_train = CV_full_preds_train
  )
  
  purrr::map_dfr(vims, function(vim) {
    
    output <- switch(
      vim,
      brier = survML::vim_brier(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = CV_full_preds,
        fs_hat = CV_reduced_preds,
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      ),
      AUC = survML::vim_AUC(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = purrr::map(CV_full_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        fs_hat = purrr::map(CV_reduced_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      )
    )
    
    output %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(
        vim = vim,
        indx = char_indx
      )
  })
})

kable(vim_stackG_scen2 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "AUC")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large AUC","small AUC"))

kable(vim_stackG_scen2 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "brier")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large Brier","small Brier"))
```

### Cox-Aalen model

```{r}
V0_cox_aalen_preds_scen2 <- CV_generate_full_predictions_landmark(
  time = time,
  event = event,
  X = X,
  landmark_times = landmark_times,
  approx_times = approx_times,
  nuisance = "cox.aalen",
  cf_folds = cf_folds
  )
# str(V0_cox_aalen_preds_scen2)

CV_full_preds <- V0_cox_aalen_preds_scen2$CV_full_preds
CV_full_preds_train <- V0_cox_aalen_preds_scen2$CV_full_preds_train
CV_S_preds <- V0_cox_aalen_preds_scen2$CV_S_preds
CV_G_preds <- V0_cox_aalen_preds_scen2$CV_G_preds

indxs <- 1:ncol(X)
vim_cox_aalen_scen2 <- purrr::map_dfr(indxs, function(indx_i) {
  
  char_indx <- as.character(indx_i)
  indx <- as.numeric(strsplit(char_indx, split = ",")[[1]])
  
  CV_reduced_preds <- CV_generate_reduced_predictions_landmark(
    time = time,
    event = event,
    X = X,
    landmark_times = landmark_times,
    cf_folds = cf_folds,
    indx = indx,
    full_preds_train = CV_full_preds_train
  )
  
  purrr::map_dfr(vims, function(vim) {
    
    output <- switch(
      vim,
      brier = survML::vim_brier(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = CV_full_preds,
        fs_hat = CV_reduced_preds,
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      ),
      AUC = survML::vim_AUC(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = purrr::map(CV_full_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        fs_hat = purrr::map(CV_reduced_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      )
    )
    
    output %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(
        vim = vim,
        indx = char_indx
      )
  })
})

kable(vim_cox_aalen_scen2 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "AUC")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large AUC","small AUC"))

kable(vim_cox_aalen_scen2 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "brier")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large Brier","small Brier"))
```

### Survival Super Learner 

```{r eval=FALSE}
V0_survivalSL_preds_scen2 <- CV_generate_full_predictions_landmark(
  time = time,
  event = event,
  X = X,
  landmark_times = landmark_times,
  approx_times = approx_times,
  nuisance = "survivalSL",
  cf_folds = cf_folds)
# str(V0_stackG_preds_scen2)

CV_full_preds <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen2$CV_full_preds
CV_full_preds_train <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen2$CV_full_preds_train
CV_S_preds <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen2$CV_S_preds
CV_G_preds <- V0_survivalSL_preds_scen2$CV_G_preds

indxs <- 1:ncol(X)
vim_survivalSL_scen2 <- purrr::map_dfr(indxs, function(indx_i) {
  
  char_indx <- as.character(indx_i)
  indx <- as.numeric(strsplit(char_indx, split = ",")[[1]])
  
  CV_reduced_preds <- CV_generate_reduced_predictions_landmark(
    time = time,
    event = event,
    X = X,
    landmark_times = landmark_times,
    cf_folds = cf_folds,
    indx = indx,
    full_preds_train = CV_full_preds_train
  )
  
  purrr::map_dfr(vims, function(vim) {
    
    output <- switch(
      vim,
      brier = survML::vim_brier(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = CV_full_preds,
        fs_hat = CV_reduced_preds,
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      ),
      AUC = survML::vim_AUC(
        time = time,
        event = event,
        approx_times = approx_times,
        landmark_times = landmark_times,
        f_hat = purrr::map(CV_full_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        fs_hat = purrr::map(CV_reduced_preds, ~ 1 - .x),
        S_hat = CV_S_preds,
        G_hat = CV_G_preds,
        cf_folds = cf_folds,
        sample_split = sample_split, 
        ss_folds = ss_folds
      )
    )
    
    output %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(
        vim = vim,
        indx = char_indx
      )
  })
})

kable(vim_survivalSL_scen2 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "AUC")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large AUC","small AUC"))

kable(vim_survivalSL_scen2 %>% 
        dplyr::filter(vim == "brier")%>%
        tibble::remove_rownames()%>%
        mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>%
        dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>%
        dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time),
      col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large Brier","small Brier"))
```


<!-- # Additional numerical experiments  -->

<!-- ## Sensitivity to low-prevalence covariates  -->

<!-- ### Objectives -->

<!-- This scenario aims to evaluate the overall performance of the VIM procedure to ***rare alterations*** exhibiting ***heterogeneous effect sizes (ranging from weak to strong)*** under an additive ***Aalen model*** and a mixed additive and multiplicative ***Cox-Aaalen model***. More specifically, the objective is to assess whether the VIM approach can: -->

<!-- * detect rare yet highly prognostic alterations, -->
<!-- * discriminate between weak and strong prognostic signals, and -->
<!-- * correctly identify non-informative (null) variables. -->

<!-- ### Data generating mechanism -->

<!-- All covariates were generated independently as binary variables:  -->

<!-- $$X_j \sim \mathcal{B}(\pi_j)$$ with $\pi \in {\{0.01, 0.1\}}$ to reflect genomic rarity.  -->

<!-- The event times follows a proportional hazards model with a with constant baseline hazard $\lambda_0(t) = 0.1$ such that -->

<!-- $$ -->
<!-- \lambda(t \mid X) = \lambda_0(t)\exp(X^\top \beta). -->
<!-- $$ -->

<!-- Covariates were partitioned according to both their prevalence and prognostic effect size, resulting in the following combinations: -->

<!-- ```{r echo=FALSE} -->
<!-- knitr::kable( -->
<!--   data.frame( -->
<!--     `Alteration frequency` = c( -->
<!--       rep("Very rare ($\\pi = 0.01$)", 5), -->
<!--       rep("Rare ($\\pi = 0.10$)", 5) -->
<!--     ), -->
<!--     `Effect category` = c( -->
<!--       "Strong", "Strong", "Moderate", "Weak", "Null", -->
<!--       "Strong", "Strong", "Moderate", "Weak", "Null" -->
<!--     ), -->
<!--     `Hazard ratio` = c( -->
<!--       3.0, 2.0, 1.5, 1.2, 1.0, -->
<!--       3.0, 2.0, 1.5, 1.2, 1.0 -->
<!--     ), -->
<!--     `Regression coefficient` = c( -->
<!--       "$\\log(3)$", -->
<!--       "$\\log(2)$", -->
<!--       "$\\log(1.5)$", -->
<!--       "$\\log(1.2)$", -->
<!--       "$0$", -->
<!--       "$\\log(3)$", -->
<!--       "$\\log(2)$", -->
<!--       "$\\log(1.5)$", -->
<!--       "$\\log(1.2)$", -->
<!--       "$0$" -->
<!--     ) -->
<!--   ), -->
<!--   escape = FALSE, -->
<!--   align = "llcc", -->
<!--   col.names = c("Alteration frequency","Effect category","HR","$\\beta$") -->
<!-- ) -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- This design allows us to assess whether the VIM procedure can detect rare but strongly prognostic alterations, distinguish weak from moderate and strong effects, and correctly assign low importance to null variables. -->

<!-- Independent censoring times were generated as $C \sim Uniform(0, c_{max})$. The parameter $c_{max}$ was calibrated using a large Monte Carlo sample from the data-generating mechanism to achieve an expected censoring proportion of approximately 20%, and the calibrated value was then fixed across all  -->
<!-- simulation replicates.  -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- p <- 10  -->
<!-- prev <- c(rep(0.01, 5), rep(0.1, 5)) -->
<!-- beta <- rep(c(log(3), log(2), log(1.5), log(1.2), 0), 2) -->
<!-- sigma <- diag(x = 1, nrow = p, ncol = p) -->
<!-- lambda0 <- 0.1 -->
<!-- target_cens <- 0.2 -->
<!-- c_max_scen3 <- calibrate_c_max_cox(lambda0     = lambda0, -->
<!--                                    beta        = beta, -->
<!--                                    prev        = prev, -->
<!--                                    sigma       = sigma, -->
<!--                                    target_cens = target_cens, -->
<!--                                    seed        = 2026) -->
<!-- c_max_scen3 -->
<!-- data_scen3 <- simulate_cox_data(n, prev, sigma, lambda0, beta, c_max_scen3) -->
<!-- head(data_scen3) -->

<!-- # Check empirical prevalences -->
<!-- round(colMeans(data_scen3 %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("X"))), 3) -->

<!-- # Check censoring -->
<!-- mean(1 - data_scen3$event) -->

<!-- # Cox PH model -->
<!-- coxph_fit <- coxph(Surv(time, event)~., data_scen3) -->
<!-- summary(coxph_fit) -->

<!-- time <- data_scen3$time -->
<!-- event <- data_scen3$event -->
<!-- X <- data_scen3 %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("X")) -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ### Variable importance  -->

<!-- We define time horizons, maximum follow-up time and times at which to approximate integrals. -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- landmark_times <- quantile(time, c(0.25, 0.75)) -->
<!-- approx_times <- sort(unique(c(0, time[event == 1], landmark_times))) -->
<!-- approx_times <- approx_times[approx_times <= max(landmark_times)] -->
<!-- length(approx_times) -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- We generated cross-fitting and sample-splitting folds. -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- folds <- survML:::generate_folds(n, V, sample_split) -->
<!-- cf_folds <- folds$cf_folds -->
<!-- table(cf_folds) -->
<!-- ss_folds <- folds$ss_folds -->
<!-- table(ss_folds) -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- V0_stackG_preds_scen3 <- CV_generate_full_predictions_landmark( -->
<!--   time = time, -->
<!--   event = event, -->
<!--   X = X, -->
<!--   landmark_times = landmark_times, -->
<!--   approx_times = approx_times, -->
<!--   nuisance = "stackG", -->
<!--   cf_folds = cf_folds) -->
<!-- # str(V0_stackG_preds_scen3) -->

<!-- CV_full_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen3$CV_full_preds -->
<!-- CV_full_preds_train <- V0_stackG_preds_scen3$CV_full_preds_train -->
<!-- CV_S_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen3$CV_S_preds -->
<!-- CV_G_preds <- V0_stackG_preds_scen3$CV_G_preds -->

<!-- indxs <- 1:ncol(X) -->
<!-- vim_stackG_scen3 <- purrr::map_dfr(indxs, function(indx_i) { -->

<!--   char_indx <- as.character(indx_i) -->
<!--   indx <- as.numeric(strsplit(char_indx, split = ",")[[1]]) -->

<!--   CV_reduced_preds <- CV_generate_reduced_predictions_landmark( -->
<!--     time = time, -->
<!--     event = event, -->
<!--     X = X, -->
<!--     landmark_times = landmark_times, -->
<!--     cf_folds = cf_folds, -->
<!--     indx = indx, -->
<!--     full_preds_train = CV_full_preds_train -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   purrr::map_dfr(vims, function(vim) { -->

<!--     output <- switch( -->
<!--       vim, -->
<!--       brier = survML::vim_brier( -->
<!--         time = time, -->
<!--         event = event, -->
<!--         approx_times = approx_times, -->
<!--         landmark_times = landmark_times, -->
<!--         f_hat = CV_full_preds, -->
<!--         fs_hat = CV_reduced_preds, -->
<!--         S_hat = CV_S_preds, -->
<!--         G_hat = CV_G_preds, -->
<!--         cf_folds = cf_folds, -->
<!--         sample_split = sample_split,  -->
<!--         ss_folds = ss_folds -->
<!--       ), -->
<!--       AUC = survML::vim_AUC( -->
<!--         time = time, -->
<!--         event = event, -->
<!--         approx_times = approx_times, -->
<!--         landmark_times = landmark_times, -->
<!--         f_hat = purrr::map(CV_full_preds, ~ 1 - .x), -->
<!--         fs_hat = purrr::map(CV_reduced_preds, ~ 1 - .x), -->
<!--         S_hat = CV_S_preds, -->
<!--         G_hat = CV_G_preds, -->
<!--         cf_folds = cf_folds, -->
<!--         sample_split = sample_split,  -->
<!--         ss_folds = ss_folds -->
<!--       ) -->
<!--     ) -->

<!--     output %>%  -->
<!--       dplyr::mutate( -->
<!--         vim = vim, -->
<!--         indx = char_indx -->
<!--       ) -->
<!--   }) -->
<!-- }) -->

<!-- kable(vim_stackG_scen3 %>%  -->
<!--         dplyr::filter(vim == "AUC")%>% -->
<!--         tibble::remove_rownames()%>% -->
<!--         mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>% -->
<!--         dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>% -->
<!--         dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time), -->
<!--       col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large AUC","small AUC")) -->

<!-- kable(vim_stackG_scen3 %>%  -->
<!--         dplyr::filter(vim == "brier")%>% -->
<!--         tibble::remove_rownames()%>% -->
<!--         mutate(across(c(landmark_time:small_predictiveness), ~round(.x, 3)))%>% -->
<!--         dplyr::select(-c(cil_1sided, vim))%>% -->
<!--         dplyr::relocate(indx, landmark_time), -->
<!--       col.names = c("Variable","Horizon","VIM","var(VIM)","CIL","CIU","p","large Brier","small Brier")) -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ## Sensitivity to covariate correlations  -->

<!-- ### Additive hazards -->

<!-- #### Global survival stacking  -->

<!-- #### Aalen model -->

<!-- ### Mixed additive and multiplicative hazards  -->

<!-- #### Global survival stacking  -->

<!-- #### Cox-Aalen model -->

<!-- ## Exclusion versus permutation variable importance -->

<!-- ### Aalen hazards -->

<!-- #### Aalen model -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- # Fit the Aalen model -->
<!-- aalen_fit <- timereg::aalen( -->
<!--   Surv(time, event)~., data = data_scen1, -->
<!--   resample.iid = 1) -->

<!-- # Model performance  -->
<!-- aalen_exp <- survex::explain( -->
<!--   aalen_fit, data = data_scen1[,c("X1","X2","X3","X4")], -->
<!--   y = Surv(data_scen1$time, data_scen1$event), label = "aalen") -->
<!-- aalen_perf <- model_performance( -->
<!--   aalen_exp,  -->
<!--   metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc,  -->
<!--               `Brier score` = brier_score) -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- plot(aalen_perf) -->

<!-- times <- quantile(data_scen1$time, c(0.25, 0.75)) -->
<!-- aalen_perf_times <- model_performance( -->
<!--   aalen_exp,  -->
<!--   metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc,  -->
<!--               `Brier score` = brier_score),  -->
<!--   times = times) -->
<!-- aalen_perf_times$result -->

<!-- # Variable importance -->
<!-- permut_aalen_brier <- model_parts( -->
<!--   aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference", -->
<!--   loss_function = survex::loss_brier_score -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- plot(permut_aalen_brier) -->
<!-- # model_parts( -->
<!-- #   aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference", -->
<!-- #   loss_function = survex::loss_brier_score, -->
<!-- #   times = times -->
<!-- #   ) -->
<!-- permut_aalen_auc <- model_parts( -->
<!--   aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference", -->
<!--   loss_function = survex::loss_one_minus_cd_auc -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- plot(permut_aalen_auc) -->
<!-- # model_parts( -->
<!-- #   aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference", -->
<!-- #   loss_function = survex::loss_one_minus_cd_auc, -->
<!-- #   times = times  -->
<!-- #   ) -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- #### Random survival forests -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- # Fit random survival forests -->
<!-- rfsrc_fit <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(Surv(time, event)~., data_scen1,  -->
<!--                                     importance = "none", -->
<!--                                     splitrule = "logrank", -->
<!--                                     seed = 2024) -->

<!-- # Model performance  -->
<!-- rfsrc_exp <- survex::explain(rfsrc_fit, data = data_scen1[,c("X1","X2","X3","X4")], -->
<!--                              y = Surv(data_scen1$time, data_scen1$event)) -->
<!-- rfsrc_perf <- model_performance( -->
<!--   rfsrc_exp,  -->
<!--   metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc,  -->
<!--               `Brier score` = brier_score) -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- plot(rfsrc_perf) -->

<!-- rfsrc_perf_times <- model_performance( -->
<!--   rfsrc_exp,  -->
<!--   metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc,  -->
<!--               `Brier score` = brier_score),  -->
<!--   times = times -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- rfsrc_perf_times$result -->

<!-- # Variable importance  -->
<!-- permut_rfsrc_brier <- model_parts( -->
<!--   rfsrc_exp, B = 10, type = "difference", -->
<!--   loss_function = survex::loss_brier_score -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- plot(permut_rfsrc_brier)  -->
<!-- permut_rfsrc_auc <- model_parts( -->
<!--   rfsrc_exp, B = 10, type = "difference", -->
<!--   loss_function = survex::loss_one_minus_cd_auc -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- plot(permut_rfsrc_auc) -->
<!-- ``` -->
